mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures that trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in real GDP and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in aggregate demand that depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in GDPC1 and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns, breaking the positive supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy increases discount rates and reduces risk appetite, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms strengthening labor demand that boosts consumer spending forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real personal spending directly expand corporate revenue streams and improve earnings growth trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 110 during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 110 during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 58 percent of cases, invalidating the supply bottleneck transmission mechanism.
